For the API layer, I'm building endpoints to check migration status and apply the idempotent migration — the GET endpoint reports the last run state and verifies the tables exist, while POST re-runs the fixed migration safely since it's embedded SQL with no arbitrary execution risk.

I'm adding a confirmation header requirement (`X-IVX-Migration-Confirm: CONFIRM_CANONICAL_MEDIA_MIGRATION`) to the POST endpoint to prevent accidental triggers, since the UPDATE statements are already guarded and idempotent anyway.

Next, I'm updating the media retrieval logic to read from the canonical tables first, then fall back to legacy data if those tables are missing or empty for a project — the response will flag which source was used, and there's an environment flag to disable the fallback entirely if needed.
